description_long: This object is a semi-automatic handgun with a matte black metal slide and barrel, paired with a brown textured grip. The frame is robust and angular, with a visible trigger guard and a detachable magazine partially ejected from the grip. The magazine is black with a flat base and a visible copper-colored cartridge at the top. The slide features fine serrations at the rear for improved grip and has engraved markings on the left side. The overall design is classic, reminiscent of the M1911 style, with a prominent hammer, beavertail safety, and a squared-off trigger guard.
description: A matte black semi-automatic handgun with a brown textured grip and a partially ejected magazine showing a copper cartridge. The slide has engraved markings and rear serrations.
description_view_0: Front view showing the muzzle opening and the top of the magazine with a visible cartridge.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the full profile, brown grip, slide serrations, and engraved markings.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the hammer, beavertail safety, and the back of the grip and magazine.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the grip texture, magazine release, and the magazine partially ejected.
attribute_grip_design: The grip is rectangular with rounded edges and features a brown, checkered texture for enhanced traction. It appears to be made of wood or a wood-like polymer, with visible screw fasteners securing it to the frame. The checkering pattern covers most of the grip surface, providing a classic look and improved ergonomics. There are no visible finger grooves or logos, and the grip is slightly contoured to fit comfortably in the hand.
attribute_trigger_guard_style: The trigger guard is rounded and of medium thickness, providing ample space for finger placement. It is smoothly contoured with no undercuts or squared-off edges, and there are no visible texturing or reinforcement features. The design is simple and functional, consistent with traditional handgun styles.
attribute_barrel_type: The barrel is of standard length, extending slightly beyond the slide, with a smooth, non-threaded profile. There are no visible ports, compensators, or fluting. The exterior finish matches the matte black of the slide, and no special markings or features are present on the barrel itself.
attribute_slide_markings: The left side of the slide features engraved markings, including what appears to be a manufacturer name, model designation, and possibly a serial number. The engravings are subtle but visible, located just above the trigger area and extending toward the rear serrations. The style is utilitarian, with no decorative patterns.
attribute_magazine_base_shape: The magazine base is flat and rectangular, with slightly beveled edges for easier insertion and removal. It is black and lacks any finger rest, flaring, or visible logos. The design is minimalistic, focusing on function over embellishment.
